codeReview.summary: The fix removes the two unsound compatibility shortcuts introduced by the prior generic Self repair while preserving the targeted generic Self constructor and chained-call behavior. `types_compatible` now gates `TParam` wildcarding on in-scope generic names, rejects bare-vs-instantiated nominal generic matches unless arity and arguments line up, and adds regression coverage for the previously missing negative cases alongside the preserved positive generic Self tests.

summary: Reviewed the fix handoff, transcript skeleton, prior failed review, and both commits (`6a0fb86` and `1323fae`). The new implementation closes the prior soundness hole where out-of-scope `TParam` values behaved as universal wildcards, removes the global bare-vs-instantiated nominal generic compatibility shortcut, preserves the earlier generic `Self` constructor/method-chain regressions, and adds focused negative tests for the original failure modes. I found no new correctness regressions or missing coverage within the reviewed scope.
